page: Google Cloud Platform ct5p-hightpu-4t is a specialized accelerator-optimized server instance belonging to the ct5p family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 208 dedicated virtual CPUs and 448.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields approximately 2.15 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with four integrated Google TPUs of the ct5p model to handle accelerated mathematical computations.

This server type does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage options. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile is highly dense in compute resources and specialized accelerators, but has a relatively low memory-to-core ratio of 2.15 GB per vCPU, which represents a design tradeoff favoring raw parallel processing over memory-intensive general-purpose workloads.

The ct5p-hightpu-4t is engineered specifically for workloads that can leverage Google TPU hardware. This includes large-scale machine learning model training, deep learning inference, and complex data science pipelines. The high vCPU count of 208 dedicated cores supports intensive data preprocessing and input pipeline management to keep the four TPUs saturated.
description: Google Cloud Platform ct5p-hightpu-4t is an accelerator-optimized server instance designed for high-throughput parallel computing. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 208 dedicated vCPUs and 448.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.15 GB of RAM per core. The primary hardware feature is the inclusion of four integrated Google ct5p TPUs, which are specialized application-specific integrated circuits designed to accelerate machine learning workloads. The instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external network-attached storage solutions. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for specialized parallel processing tasks compared to general-purpose compute resources. It is optimally suited for deep learning model training, large-scale neural network inference, and advanced data science workloads that directly utilize TPU acceleration.

tagline: An accelerator-optimized instance featuring two hundred eight dedicated cores and four integrated tensor processing units for machine learning workloads.

448.0 GB of system memory providing 2.15 GB of RAM per core
